events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the required JSON directly to `/app/answer.json`. The observed failed micron decay means the evacuation has not passed the required leak/moisture integrity check, so this should be treated as non-compliant until corrected and retested.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 85,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.92,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Vacuum decay test did not hold at the target micron level.\",
    \"System cannot be accepted as dry, tight, and ready for refrigerant charge.\",
    \"Likely leak, trapped moisture, contaminated oil, or defective brazed/mechanical joint remains in the installed piping assembly.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Do not release system for refrigerant charging or closeout.\",
    \"Isolate and verify micron gauge, hoses, core removal tools, and pump performance before retesting.\",
    \"Pressure test and leak-check the circuit, repair defective brazed or mechanical joints, replace contaminated filter driers as required, then re-evacuate.\",
    \"Perform standing vacuum decay test to the manufacturer-specified target microns and document passing results before charging.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"A refrigeration circuit that will not hold the specified evacuation level during decay testing fails the required dehydration and tightness verification. At rough-complete stage this is correctable, but the installed work is not compliant with manufacturer evacuation requirements, accepted HVAC-R practice, or refrigerant handling obligations until the cause is found, repaired, evacuated, and documented as passing.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"Manufacturer installation and startup specifications for evacuation target microns and decay/standing vacuum acceptance criteria\",
    \"ACCA HVAC-R accepted practices for leak testing, evacuation, dehydration, and commissioning before refrigerant charging\",
    \"EPA Section 608 refrigerant handling requirements requiring leak prevention and proper service practices before charging\"
  ]
}
EOF
jq . /app/answer.json"
→ /bin/sh: 34: jq: not found
